---
title: "Data and Privacy"
---

# Data and Privacy

Mayon is designed around a local-first principle. Your data stays on your machine. This page explains how storage, API keys, and offline capabilities work.

## Storage

Application data is stored in a PostgreSQL database running in a Docker container. Data persists in the `pg-data` docker volume across container restarts and updates.

### Migrations

Migrations are managed by drizzle-kit. The server runs drizzle's native `migrate()` at boot. If the database is empty (first run), all migrations apply sequentially. If it already exists, only new migrations run.

## API key storage

API keys are never written to the `settings` table. The settings table holds only provider configuration metadata (base URL, model, template type).

**Browser:** Keys are stored in IndexedDB, which is scoped to the same origin as the app. The key is never transmitted anywhere except the provider's API endpoint during streaming (or the Mayon server's CORS proxy, which is same-origin and transient).

## No telemetry

Mayon does not collect, transmit, or report any telemetry. There are no analytics, no usage tracking, and no phone-home mechanisms. The app does not make any network requests except to the AI providers you explicitly configure.

## Offline capability

The app requires the Mayon server to be running. Chat, branching, labs, and quizzes all work as long as the server and Postgres are available.

The only feature that requires internet access is AI streaming (sending prompts to a provider and receiving responses). If you use a local provider like Ollama, even AI streaming works without internet access.
